title: Windows bind support for UNC WSL paths
body:
### Issue Description

On Windows, I cannot run a container that binds to a directory in WSL2:

cd \\\\wsl.localhost\podman-machine-default\home\user
podman run -it -v .:/mydir ubuntu:latest
Error: getting absolute path of .: unsupported UNC path

Also this doesn't work:
cd c:\\
podman run -it -v \\wsl.localhost\podman-machine-default\home\user:/mydir ubuntu:latest
Error: getting absolute path of \\\\wsl.localhost\podman-machine-default\home\user: unsupported UNC path

Same thing using podman for linux:
podman machine ssh
podman run -it -v .:/foo ubuntu:latest
Error: getting absolute path of .: unsupported UNC path


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. cd \\\\wsl.localhost\podman-machine-default\home\user
3. podman run -it -v .:/mydir ubuntu:latest

Error: getting absolute path of .: unsupported UNC path

Also:
1. cd c:\\
2. podman run -it -v \\\\wsl.localhost\podman-machine-default\home\user:/mydir ubuntu:latest

Error: getting absolute path of \\\\wsl.localhost\podman-machine-default\home\user: unsupported UNC path


### Describe the results you received

Error: getting absolute path of .: unsupported UNC path

### Describe the results you expected

Podman should be able to mount a path within WSL

comments.body: @n1hility PTAL

You can bind a directory on the WSL instance by using its fully qualified unix path:

`podman run -it -v /home/user/dir:/mydir <container name>`

Feel free to reopen if this doesnt work for you

Thanks!

hi @n1hility 
I know it works using the unix path, but docker accepts also the windows one.
This prevents me to use podman as a drop-in replacement for docker.

In particular, I have this issue with CLion for Windows:
https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/CPP-32837/Docker-toolchain-with-podman-and-project-sources-in-WSL-filesystem
In this use case it is crucial to mount a linux directory because the performance of the fs access is too bad otherwise
Ah ok. Thanks for clarifying your use case. We can add support for these paths
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
...also visual studio code devcontainers.
This is actually the only thing missing if I want to combine CLion on Windows with podman instead of docker with WSLv2. Would be great if this could be implemented.

I am getting this error when trying to open a devcontainer in Windows. I would love to be able to use Podman but this is stopping me from being able to do so. I am a maintainer of dbachecks so am using the [devcontainer here](https://github.com/dataplat/dbachecks/blob/main/.devcontainer/devcontainer.json) with the [docker-compose here](https://github.com/dataplat/dbachecks/blob/main/.devcontainer/docker-compose.yml. Images are downloaded and dbachecks2 and dbachecks3 run but dbachecks1 fails with 
```
Creating dbachecks1 ... error

ERROR: for dbachecks1  Cannot create container for service dbachecks1: fill out specgen: getting absolute path of \\wsl.localhost\Ubuntu-22.04\run\user\1000
\wayland-0: unsupported UNC path
```

Windows11 WSL 2 Ubuntu 22.04
Is there any progress on this? This practically stops you from using Podman to run VSCode devcontainers, which is a real bummer.

I think the fix shouldn't be too complicated.
When we run in  to this error, we need to check if the path starts with `\\wsl$\<podman-machine>\...` and just translate that to the root directory `/`.
Since there might be more than a single machine, we need to know which podman-machine currently runs. This information must be available somewhere. 
Could it then be enough to extend the switch statement at https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/348f9fe0d9aa3b636049a1234e32f26ee2c46309/pkg/specgen/winpath.go#L49 with an additional case:
```
// pseudocode since I'm not a go developer and I don't yet know how to get the machineName
case strings.HasPrefix(path, `\\wsl$\` + machineName ):
    path = "/" + path[7 + length(machineName)];
```

Could `Name` in https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/machine/config.go#L31 be the right thing to use and import here?

> You can bind a directory on the WSL instance by using its fully qualified unix path:
> 
> `podman run -it -v /home/user/dir:/mydir <container name>`

Do you run this commnad from windows or wsl?

When running from windows, the home directory is the one in podman machine, not in default wsl home. I want to bind the directory in default wsl environment.
When running from wsl, `podman` is not found. Do you mean to install podman in default wsl environment?

Compare to docker, when running `docker` command in wsl, there is no need to install docker in wsl. The executable seems to be from docker desktop.
```
readlink -f /usr/bin/docker
/mnt/wsl/docker-desktop/cli-tools/usr/bin/docker
```

Is there any update on this issue?
I want to use podman for VS Code devcontainers and got the same issue.

Just a "me too" on. This still breaks many VSCODE-dev containers. here little more than a year after the initial report.

@raist246 did you ever manage to turn https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17986#issuecomment-1721044478 into a patch?

I'm also running into this while trying to use podman with VSCode dev containers.

> You can bind a directory on the WSL instance by using its fully qualified unix path:
> 
> `podman run -it -v /home/user/dir:/mydir <container name>`

Hey @n1hility, I'm not sure if that would work? I've got a Ubuntu WSL alongside my `podman-machine-default` WSL. Doing something like `podman run -it -v /home/user/example:/example debian:bookworm bash` errors with:
```
Error: statfs /home/user/example: no such file or directory
```
even though `ls /home/user/example` is a real directory. I'm not sure how to mount files from my own WSL into a docker container on Podman. The paths provided are assumed to be Podman WSL paths, not my preferred WSL.

This isn't a fundamental solution, but if you only need to use VS Code's Dev Containers, you can try disabling the "Mount Wayland Socket" option. By doing so, the socket won't be mounted, and the container should be able to start.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c5942d25-c0f1-4155-b46e-a2ce138149f0)

https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-remote-release/issues/8157#issuecomment-1483099183
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-remote-release/issues/8157#issuecomment-1485704718
Same issue with devcontainers. Modifying the suggested setting (`Mount wayland socket`) worked but I'd like to avoid custom settings if possible.

I have been using VS Code dev containers with Podman for about a year. In that time, I have worked on a couple different Windows laptops. I have never run into this issue personally, but I was just helping another dev set up dev containers and he had this issue. This led me to wonder why I haven't run into it before, but this other dev did. Obviously, it doesn't affect all computers. The suggested work around of disabling the _mount wayland socket_ setting in VS Code allowed him to work around the issue. Have we identified the root of the issue?

I have the same problem, and wayland socket set to false also does not work. 

Does anyone have any other suggestions?

OS: windows10 22H2 , Build 19045.5608
podman desktop: v1.17.2

Note: Runs on WSL2 distro ubuntu 24.04

I ran into this issue with vscode devcontainers on windows.
```
fill out specgen: getting absolute path of \\wsl.localhost\podman-machine-default\mnt\wslg\runtime-dir\wayland-0: unsupported UNC path.
```

It occurred because I tried to do X11 forwarding setup. Here's the relevant log lines (truncated for visibility:
```log
[2025-05-15T05:10:49.318Z] Start: Run in container: node /root/.vscode-remote-containers/dist/dev-containers-cli-0.413.0/dist/spec-node/devContainersSpecCLI.js up --container-session-data-folder /tmp/devcontainers-0c6dde6e-208c-4ec4-9a54-a309637944861747285841488 --workspace-folder /workspaces/vscode-powershell --workspace-mount-consistency cached --gpu-availability detect --id-label vsch.local.repository=https://github.com/PowerShell/vscode-powershell.git/tree/build/devcontainer --id-label vsch.local.repository.volume=vsc-remote-containers --id-label vsch.local.repository.folder=vscode-powershell --id-label devcontainer.config_file=/workspaces/vscode-powershell/.devcontainer/devcontainer.json --log-level debug --log-format json --config /workspaces/vscode-powershell/.devcontainer/devcontainer.json --override-config /tmp/devcontainer-3d6ab36b-3d68-4fe3-86ed-e63f907c9d78.json --default-user-env-probe loginInteractiveShell --mount type=volume,source=vsc-remote-containers,target=/workspaces,external=true --mount type=volume,source=vscode,target=/vscode,external=true --mount type=bind,source=\\wsl.localhost\podman-machine-default\mnt\wslg\runtime-dir\wayland-0,target=/tmp/vscode-wayland-63307b84-39fd-47fd-ae2a-f77ffc7cb800.sock --skip-post-create 
[2025-05-15T05:11:00.162Z] Start: Starting container
[2025-05-15T05:11:00.162Z] Start: Run: docker run --sig-proxy=false -a STDOUT -a STDERR --mount type=volume,src=vsc-remote-containers,dst=/workspaces --mount type=volume,src=vscode,dst=/vscode --mount type=bind,src=\\wsl.localhost\podman-machine-default\mnt\wslg\runtime-dir\wayland-0,dst=/tmp/vscode-wayland-63307b84-39fd-47fd-ae2a-f77ffc7cb800.sock -l vsch.local.repository=https://github.com/PowerShell/vscode-powershell.git/tree/build/devcontainer -l vsch.local.repository.volume=vsc-remote-containers -l vsch.local.repository.folder=vscode-powershell -l devcontainer.config_file=/workspaces/vscode-powershell/.devcontainer/devcontainer.json --entrypoint /bin/sh vsc-vscode-powershell-c9422c9f370d8a452a9f3ca8827ebc0970d66df23329e44fcb5bf0daccadd4c8 -c echo Container started
[2025-05-15T05:11:00.205Z] docker: Error response from daemon: fill out specgen: getting absolute path of \\wsl.localhost\podman-machine-default\mnt\wslg\runtime-dir\wayland-0: unsupported UNC path.
See 'docker run --help'.
```

Unsetting `Forward Wayland Socket` resolves the issue as mentioned.
Podman would really benefit by fixing this because it enables better VSCode devcontainer integration. A huge use-case for Windows users which want to get rid of Docker (Desktop).

I also ran into this when migrating from Docker Desktop to Podman on Windows. Strangely enough, initially my DevContainer worked in Podman but after I made some minor adjustments to the DevContainer configuration and rebuilt it, then this error occurred.

The workaround of disabling mounting the wayland socket also worked for me.

> I also ran into this when migrating from Docker Desktop to Podman on Windows. Strangely enough, initially my DevContainer worked in Podman but after I made some minor adjustments to the DevContainer configuration and rebuilt it, then this error occurred.
> 
> The workaround of disabling mounting the wayland socket also worked for me.

I had the exact same problem. It worked at first, then stopped.
Mount Wayland Socket OFF works.

After disabling "Mount Wayland Socket" I was able to start the dev container on Windows.

Is that a problem of Podman or of DevContainers?

This issues triggers https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-remote-release/issues/8157 for me. Having this issue solved would enable devcontainers to work in VS Code without first having to google what default setting to disable.

Furthermore, it would likely enable Wayland support via WSL2 on Windows in Podman.
